expectations.text: References the existing feature-dev skill directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Quotes Phase 5 step 3 verbatim ('Always use the tdd skill to implement with an explicit red-green-refactor loop'), quotes Phase 6 step 1 ('Always run independent code-reviewer agents'), and references the Light Track opening sentence from the actual skill file.

expectations.text: Avoids asking blocking clarifying questions for this well-specified task.
expectations.evidence: Open Questions section states 'None blocking. The request is concrete enough to implement directly.' Closes with an offer to apply, not a blocking question.

expectations.text: Proposes concrete description and body changes, including a scoped 'When not to use this skill' addition.
expectations.evidence: Edit 1 provides a full before/after description replacement. Edit 2 inserts a dedicated 'When not to use this skill' section with a scoped exclusion list before Core Principles.

expectations.text: Scales the workflow to the task instead of insisting on a full multi-phase approval process.
expectations.evidence: Declares 'Track: Light' at the top; presents four focused edits in a single response; ends with 'Ready to apply immediately on your go-ahead — or I can do it now'. No multi-phase approval gate.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires tdd for implementation, code-simplifier as a subagent for refactoring review, and independent subagent-based quality review.
expectations.evidence: Edit 4 promotes TDD to a bold 'Required' block at the top of Phase 5 and adds step 3 launching a code-simplifier subagent as a mandatory refactor pass after green tests. Independent quality review is noted as already present and explicit in Phase 6 step 1.
